functions.//: The Express backend (backend/) deployed as a single 2nd-gen HTTPS function named `api`. predeploy runs the local TypeScript build (tsc && tsc-alias) so dist/ holds plain relative-import JS; the cloud install pulls production deps only. Credentials come from the runtime service-account ADC, so .env + serviceAccountKey.json are excluded from the upload.

hosting.//: Framework-aware hosting for the Next.js frontend (client-rendered SPA with dynamic client routes; `source` lets Firebase build + deploy it). The /api/** rewrite proxies all API traffic to the Express `api` Cloud Function on the SAME origin, so the browser never makes a cross-origin call (no CORS) and Express receives the original /api/... path. Requires `firebase experiments:enable webframeworks`.

rewrites.//: Client-rendered dynamic routes: the build emits literal-bracket files (chats/[id].html, etc.). Map a deep-link / hard refresh on /chats/<id> to that shell so the client router can read the real id from the URL. Without these, direct loads of dynamic routes 404 (only in-app navigation works).
